title: Pronouncing Prepositions
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation and linking of common prepositions of place and time.
instructions: 前置詞（in, on, atなど）を含む短いフレーズを発音します。前置詞は通常、弱く・素早く発音され、次の単語とくっつく（リンキングする）ことが多いです。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Pronounce short phrases containing prepositions. Prepositions are usually spoken weakly and quickly, often linking to the next word. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: イン ザ ボックス
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Say 'in the' very quickly together, then stress 'BOX'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'in, za, bokkusu' with equal stress.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: オン ザ テーブル
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Stress 'TA-ble'. The 'on the' should be quiet and fast.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: アット ザ ステーション
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 't' in 'at' is very soft. It almost sounds like 'ah-the station'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: アンダー ザ ベッド
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Un-der. Pull your tongue back for the 'r'.
Don't say the 't' twice. Share the 't'. Nex-to.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: イン フロン トヴ
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Link the 't' in front to the 'o' in of. In fron-tov.
The 't' in 'at' is very soft or stopped. At-night.

expectedPronunciationText: in the box on the table at the station under the bed next to behind in front of in May on Monday at night

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You made the prepositions sound natural and linked them well.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Prepositions (in, on, at) should be spoken softly, while the main noun (box, table, Monday) should be loud and clear.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to share the 't' in 'next to' (nex-to).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: in the box on the table at the station under the bed next to behind in front of in May on Monday at night
acceptedVariants: in the box on the table at the station under the bed next to behind in front of in may on monday at night
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: in the box on the table at the station under the bed next to behind in front of in May on Monday at night
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: in the boks on the taybl at the stayshun under the bed nekst to bihind in frunt of in may on munday at nite

audioAssets.audioTranscript: in the box, on the table, at the station, under the bed, next to, behind, in front of, in May, on Monday, at night

hintText: Native speakers don't pronounce every word with the same volume. Prepositions are 'grammar words' and are spoken very quietly and quickly.
